description_long: This is a vintage pipe wrench-style hammer with a robust, metallic head and a wooden handle. The head features an adjustable jaw mechanism, typical of pipe wrenches, with visible gear teeth and a solid, flat striking surface. The handle is cylindrical, made of wood, and capped with a metallic ferrule at the junction with the head, showing a blue accent near the metal. The metal head is dark, with signs of oxidation and wear, giving it a rugged, well-used appearance. The tool is compact, with the handle proportionally longer than the head, and the overall build suggests it is designed for both gripping and striking. The object is utilitarian, likely found in garages, workshops, or urban maintenance settings.
description: A vintage pipe wrench-style hammer with a worn metal head and a wooden handle, featuring an adjustable jaw and visible signs of use.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded wooden handle end and the broad, flat face of the metal head with the jaw mechanism visible.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the full length of the wooden handle, the blue accent, and the detailed adjustable jaw and gear teeth of the head.
description_view_2: Rear view focuses on the rounded end of the wooden handle and the back of the head, with less detail of the jaw visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays the handle and the other side of the head, showing the adjustment mechanism and the flat striking face.
attribute_handle_type: wooden straight handle with a smooth finish and a metallic ferrule at the head junction, featuring a blue accent near the metal
attribute_head_type: pipe wrench-style head with an adjustable jaw mechanism, gear teeth, and a flat striking face
attribute_wear_or_damage_marks: visible rust, scratches, and signs of wear on the metal head; handle shows some surface wear but no major damage
